title: Normalize trace-start RNG after arm construction
description: Fix the harness-level E1 step-0 RNG mismatch caused by arm-specific constructor consumption while preserving construction provenance and all production model/optimizer semantics.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN all production arms finish construction THEN the trace records each arm's construction RNG metadata and creates one immutable trace-start RNG snapshot; WHEN step-0 initialization begins THEN host, final_v2_closed_gate_full, and global_p restore that same snapshot before validation sampling, batch probing, and checkpoint comparison; WHEN the focused regression tests run THEN a synthetic arm-specific RNG consumption case proves the three trace-start states and downstream probe states are identical without changing model parameters, optimizer membership, seed 100, trace steps, FP32 tolerance, evaluator, or corruption; HARD STOP: any missing provenance, unequal normalized RNG state, changed optimizer/parameter topology, or new remote divergence blocks downstream tasks and forbids retry/rescue tuning/threshold change; ref: scripts/run_e01_gzero_trace.py:123
review_initial_requirements: Verify the diagnosis against the archived step-0 report and production call path; confirm the fix normalizes only the trace-start boundary and does not rewrite frozen artifacts or alter model/optimizer construction.
review_regression_requirements: Verify focused tests, full E1/front-gate regressions, bundle/source hashes, and one new dated remote attempt; report pass only if the structured trace has no first divergence at steps 0,1,100,1000.

title: Align host/proposal stochastic graph kernels at g=0
description: Make AdaptiveWise and ProposalAdaptiveWise use one distribution-preserving multinomial helper so a proposal equal to the host core p1 has identical sample_nonpreference/sample_prob draws and RNG consumption, while non-constant per-row proposal probabilities retain batched sampling.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the shared sampler helper is tested on an identical row-constant proposal THEN host and proposal sample_nonpreference and sample_prob return identical tensors and leave identical RNG states under a fixed seed; WHEN a non-constant proposal matrix is tested THEN each row is sampled from its own probability vector with no flattening shortcut; WHEN deterministic rate/prob/reverse kernels receive equal p1/proposal rows THEN existing exact-value behavior remains unchanged; HARD STOP: any change to seed 100, FP32 tolerance, optimizer membership, model parameters, proposal values, evaluator, or corruption is forbidden, and a new dated remote E1 attempt must fail closed if any checkpoint diverges; ref: graph_lib.py:1203
review_initial_requirements: Verify the CPU reproduction of host torch.multinomial versus proposal sample_categorical, confirm the helper preserves proposal-specific rows, and audit that the fix is a shared production graph-kernel implementation rather than a trace-only bypass.
review_regression_requirements: Verify graph unit tests, E1/front-gate/controller regressions, source hashes, and one isolated seed-100 remote trace; report pass only if checkpoints 0,1,100,1000 contain no failed comparisons.

title: Instrument per-stage RNG provenance for E1
description: Record pre/post RNG metadata around each initial validation sampler call and its first batch so the remaining R3 step-0 RNG mismatch is localized without changing production behavior.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the diagnostic trace runs THEN initialization evidence contains per-arm RNG metadata for construction, normalized trace start, validation-loader iterator creation, each p2 sampler call, and post-evaluation capture with call counts and batch shapes; WHEN the diagnostic compares arms THEN it identifies the first stage where RNG metadata diverges and preserves all existing model/optimizer/proposal values; HARD STOP: instrumentation must be behavior-preserving, seed 100/trace steps/tolerance remain frozen, no result is treated as pass, and no downstream training launches from this diagnostic row; ref: scripts/run_e01_gzero_trace.py:1113
review_initial_requirements: Verify the diagnostic wraps the existing evaluator/sampler without consuming extra RNG or altering output, and use R3 evidence to target the stage boundary.
review_regression_requirements: Verify focused instrumentation tests, full regression suite, isolated bundle hash, and one remote diagnostic trace; classify the first divergent stage before selecting R5 fix.

title: Probe internal production sampler stages
description: Record RNG metadata around context encoding, graph initial draw, predictor categorical draws, and denoiser draw for the first validation sampler call so the R4 divergence is attributed to one concrete operation.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the stage probe runs THEN it wraps only the first p2 sampler call per arm and records before/after hashes for encode_history_context, graph.sample_nonpreference, each predictor update sample, and denoiser sample; WHEN the probe completes THEN it names the first operation whose post-state differs while preserving sampler outputs, model state, and RNG state outside the wrapper; HARD STOP: no production behavior change, seed/tolerance/trace-step change, pass marker, or training launch is allowed; ref: sampling.py:240
review_initial_requirements: Verify wrappers call originals exactly once, do not capture or restore RNG in a way that changes behavior, and distinguish context, graph, predictor, and denoiser boundaries.
review_regression_requirements: Verify stage-probe unit tests, full regressions, isolated bundle hash, and one diagnostic remote trace; select the next repair only from the named first operation.

title: Run a new dated E1 production trace attempt
description: Execute one fresh isolated production-path E1 g-zero trace after the prior attempt failed before trace entry because its bundle omitted catsample.py; preserve the prior failure as historical evidence and do not retry or overwrite it.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the new dated attempt runs with random_seed 100 THEN proposal rows, loss terms, gradients, parameters, optimizer state, ordered batch IDs, configuration, and RNG metadata are compared at trace steps 0, 1, 100, and 1000 under FP32 tolerance 1e-6; WHEN the attempt reaches a terminal state THEN exactly one dated e01_gzero_trace.json records pass or fail, first divergence, hashes, log, and isolated paths; HARD STOP: any preflight/import/execution error or first tracked difference above tolerance fails this attempt closed, creates no RISK-02 pass marker, launches no training, and permits no retry, rescue tuning, threshold change, or corruption change; only a valid pass may unlock the approved seed100 continuation path; ref: scripts/run_e01_gzero_trace.py:1
review_initial_requirements: Verify this is a genuinely new dated attempt with a complete runtime bundle including catsample.py, fixed seed/steps/tolerance, no pre-created output directory, GPU1 isolation, and no mutation of frozen artifacts.
review_regression_requirements: Verify terminal artifact identity and hashes, distinguish preflight/execution failure from scientific divergence, preserve the old attempt, and confirm downstream launch remains locked unless E1 pass plus RISK-02 marker and controller gates validate.

title: Capture graph-initial probability and layout provenance
description: Run one new isolated E1 diagnostic after R5 localized the first divergence to graph.sample_nonpreference; wrap the shared probability-row sampler only for the first p2 call and record each arm's probability input, batch shape, dtype, device, shape, stride, contiguity, numeric summary, content hash, output, and RNG before/after without changing the operation.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the R6 diagnostic runs THEN the shared _sample_probability_rows operation is called exactly once per first graph-initial call and the report records input/output provenance for host, final_v2_closed_gate_full, and global_p; WHEN it terminates THEN it identifies the first unequal probability or layout/output boundary, preserves E1 status=fail, creates no RISK-02 pass marker, and starts no training; HARD STOP: no seed/tolerance/evaluator/model/optimizer/proposal/corruption change, no RNG restore/reseed, no retry, and no overwrite; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-e01-graph-initial-provenance-design.md:1
review_initial_requirements: Use R5 terminal evidence to target only graph.sample_nonpreference and verify the wrapper calls the shared sampler once, restores it, and records all arm-specific layouts and hashes.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the new focused RED/GREEN test, full regression suite, isolated bundle and source hashes, GPU1-only diagnostic process, terminal report/manifest/log, no pass marker, and no training PID.

title: Capture AMP execution traceback and frozen runtime context
description: Run one new isolated E1 diagnostic after R6 ended with a CPU-backend AMP unscale error; record the unmodified exception traceback and the last construction, initial-sampling, training arm/step, device, scaler, and optimizer context without changing AMP or production behavior.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the R7 diagnostic runs THEN the report preserves the original exception type/message/traceback and identifies the exact phase, arm, and trace step; WHEN it terminates THEN it records downstream authorization=false, no pass marker, and no training result unless the independently valid E1 trace passes; HARD STOP: no AMP disablement, device move, optimizer/model/evaluator/seed/tolerance/corruption change, retry, or overwrite; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-e01-amp-failure-context-diagnostic-design.md:1
review_initial_requirements: Use R6's exact NotImplementedError to target only failure provenance and frozen context; verify context updates are non-random and do not intercept or replace the underlying operation.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the new RED/GREEN test, full E1 regression suite, isolated bundle/source hashes, GPU1-only run, terminal traceback/context artifact, no pass marker, no training authorization, and preserved R6 evidence.

title: Move production graph parameters to requested device
description: Repair the graph factory identified by R7 so every newly constructed graph module is moved to the requested device before optimizer construction, keeping all graph values/formulas, seed 100, optimizer membership, AMP, evaluator, and trace protocol unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the focused factory test requests a meta device THEN adaptive p1 is on meta and parameter-free proposal graphs remain constructible; WHEN the isolated R8 production trace runs THEN host graph.p1 and all optimizer parameters are on cuda:1, and steps 0,1,100,1000 are compared under FP32 tolerance 1e-6; HARD STOP: no parameter drop, AMP disablement, seed/tolerance/evaluator/model/proposal/corruption change, retry, overwrite, or pass marker on any failed comparison; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-e01-graph-device-placement-repair-design.md:1

title: Unify model-plus-graph training and EMA parameter contract
description: Repair the R8 optimizer-state divergence by making losses.get_step_fn use one explicit model-plus-canonical-graph-p1 list for clipping, EMA update, and EMA store/copy/restore, while excluding noise from EMA and preserving the optimizer's existing model+graph+noise membership.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the focused RED/GREEN test runs THEN an extra graph core parameter is passed to optimize and EMA operations exactly once while noise remains excluded; WHEN the isolated R9 production trace runs THEN steps 0,1,100,1000 compare all parameters, gradients, optimizer state, loss terms, RNG, proposals, and sampling under FP32 tolerance 1e-6; HARD STOP: no seed/tolerance/evaluator/AMP/learning-rate/model/formula/proposal/corruption change, no parameter drop, retry, overwrite, or pass marker on any failed comparison; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-e01-trainable-parameter-contract-repair-design.md:1
review_initial_requirements: Use R8 step-1 optimizer evidence and inspect losses.get_step_fn, EMA construction, and compose_optimizer_parameters; repair only the effective trainable set mismatch.
review_regression_requirements: Verify RED/GREEN parameter-contract test, full E1/graph/controller regressions, source/bundle hashes, GPU1-only R9 run, four checkpoint comparisons, no pass marker unless E1 passes, and no continuation launch on failure.

title: Make closed-gate proposal gradient-identical to core
description: Repair the R9 step-100 drift by returning p_core_full directly for the registered kernel-level closed gate (gate_dataset_scale=0 or global_p) so forward values and gradients follow the core softmax path; leave non-closed adaptive/anchor paths unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the focused RED/GREEN text-side test runs THEN the closed-gate proposal gradient equals the direct softmax(p1) gradient exactly; WHEN the isolated R10 production trace runs THEN steps 0,1,100,1000 have no failed comparisons under FP32 tolerance 1e-6; HARD STOP: no nonzero-gate change, seed/tolerance/evaluator/optimizer/EMA/AMP/model/formula/asset/corruption change, retry, overwrite, or pass marker on failure; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-e01-closed-gate-gradient-identity-repair-design.md:1
review_initial_requirements: Use R9 step-100 raw_score_entropy evidence to target only the closed-gate autograd path and verify non-closed injection modes retain anchor behavior.
review_regression_requirements: Verify RED/GREEN gradient-identity test, full text-side/E1/graph/controller regressions, source/bundle hashes, GPU1-only R10 run, four checkpoint comparisons, no pass marker unless E1 truly passes, and no continuation launch on failure.

acceptance_criteria: WHEN R11 launches THEN the dated bundle and output paths are unique, GPU1 is the only new compute device, and the runner imports from the requested bundle; WHEN the trace terminates THEN one report compares steps 0,1,100,1000 under FP32 tolerance 1e-6 and records pass/fail plus hashes; HARD STOP: no reuse of R10 paths, no retry, no seed/tolerance/evaluator/optimizer/EMA/AMP/model/proposal/corruption change, no pass marker or continuation on launcher/scientific failure; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-e01-r11-launch-wrapper-repair-design.md:1

review_regression_requirements: Verify remote unit tests, bundle/source hashes, GPU1-only launch, report/log, all four checkpoint comparisons, unique pass marker only on status=pass, and no continuation otherwise.
